### Post by zrothe on 2005-09-22
My xorg.conf:

> # /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"unix/:7100"			# local font server
	# if the local font server has problems, we can fall back on these
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"

	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/CID"
EndSection

	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc104"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ExplorerPS/2"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Buttons"		"9"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"false"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"6 7"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon 9800 Pro (R350 NH)"
	Driver		"ati"
	BusID		"PCI:2:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"VX724"
	Option		"DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Device		"ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon 9800 Pro (R350 NH)"
	Monitor		"VX724"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x640" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"

		Depth		8

	EndSubSection

		Depth		15
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x640" "640x480"
	EndSubSection

		Depth		16
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x640" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x640" "640x480"

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"Default Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666

I am installing WoW using Transgaming/Point2Play. When I run the graphics benchmark it runs glxgears and says that is fine but fails on the graphics acceleration test. It says it doesn't appear I have it setup correctly. I installed the latest driver from ATI.com. What else do I need to do? Thanks.

### Post by zrothe on 2005-09-22
[QUOTE=zrothe]My xorg.conf:

It says it doesn't appear I have it setup correctly. I installed the latest driver from ATI.com. What else do I need to do? Thanks.[/QUOTE]

### Post by mlomker on 2005-09-22
> I am installing WoW using Transgaming/Point2Play. When I run the graphics benchmark it runs glxgears and says that is fine but fails on the graphics acceleration test. It says it doesn't appear I have it setup correctly. I installed the latest driver from ATI.com. What else do I need to do? Thanks.

You are running the ATI driver, which is not 3D.  You need to switch to the fglrx driver.

You can follow [my how-to.](http://www.ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?p=348911) The process isn't always easy, but most people can get it working.

---

### Post by zrothe on 2005-09-22

You can follow [my how-to.](http://www.ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?p=348911) The process isn't always easy, but most people can get it working.[/QUOTE]
 Thanks for the reply. I'll give it a shot.
